title: The Assistant You Nurture
description: The vision of MulmoClaude: an AI assistant is not something you buy, but something you nurture. The presentation explains why an assistant needs a growth environment, the lock-in problem, and MulmoClaude's answer.

beats.text: The Assistant You Nurture. The vision of MulmoClaude begins with a simple claim: an AI assistant is not something you buy. It is something you nurture. And to grow well, it needs a growth environment.

slide.title: The Assistant **You Nurture**
slide.subtitle: An AI assistant is not something you {warning:buy}. It is something you {primary:nurture}.
slide.author: Satoshi Nakajima

beats.text: It is seven in the morning, and your assistant has already been working. Feeds updated while you slept. A bill reminder waiting. Flashcards ready. On the train, you ask about a restaurant list it keeps for you. Not some assistant. Your assistant.

image.html:
7:04 AM · on the train
Your assistant has already been working.
Not a generic model. A relationship that has accumulated over time.
7:04
📶 🔋
🎙
Podcast feeds updated
while you slept
🔔
Bill reminder waiting
threshold crossed before breakfast
🎴
Flashcards ready
updated from yesterday's notes
What was that ramen place on the Chuo line?
Ramen Kaze — Nakano. You saved it in March and rated it four stars.

beats.text: Every large technology company is chasing this future: an AI that knows the texture of your work and life, and gets more useful the longer it stays with you. The model is not the hard part. The hard part is the accumulation.

slide.quote: The model is not the hard part. The hard part is the {primary:accumulation}.
slide.author: What makes an assistant personal
slide.role: Records · preferences · corrections · workflows · small tools

beats.text: An assistant is not a device. Glasses, phones, watches, laptops, and desktops are doorways. And it is not the model either. GPT, Claude, Gemini, and their successors are engines. Powerful, but interchangeable. What makes the assistant yours is what grows around one life.

The old nature-versus-nurture distinction applies cleanly. The model is nature: what the assistant is born with. The accumulation is nurture: how it was brought up. But nurture needs an environment: memory, tools, structure, feedback, and a way to keep what it builds.

slide.title: An assistant needs a growth environment

items.title: Nature
items.description: The model: what the assistant is born with.

items.title: Environment
items.description: Memory, tools, structure, feedback, and a way to keep what it builds.

slide.footer: A harness is part of this environment, but not the whole of it.

beats.text: That is why a new assistant is supposed to be empty. No memories. No records. No applications built for you. It becomes useful through conversation: you ask, correct, add records, and shape workflows. Repeated needs harden into tools.

But nurture creates dependence. If the assistant's memory, data, workflows, and applications live inside one service provider, then the provider controls the accumulation. The longer you use it, the harder it becomes to switch. Memory is not just a feature. It is where lock-in compounds.

slide.quote: If one service provider controls the {warning:accumulation}, it controls the assistant.
slide.author: The risk of a hosted assistant
slide.role: The longer you use it, the harder it becomes to switch

beats.text: MulmoClaude is my attempt to avoid that lock-in by creating a growth environment the user owns. Its design rests on three constraints: regular users can grow small database applications, the applications and data live on the user's computer, and remote access is a window into that assistant, not a move to the cloud.

slide.title: Avoid lock-in with a user-owned growth environment

items.title: Keep apps and data local
items.description: the accumulation lives in the user's workspace

items.title: Reach it remotely
items.description: the phone is a window, not a new home

beats.text: First: regular users should be able to create small database applications without writing code. A restaurant list. An invoice tracker. A vocabulary drill. The user describes the need; the assistant turns it into a structured tool and helps it evolve.

slide.title: Database apps for an audience of one
slide.footer: Not a no-code builder. A conversation that grows a tool.

beats.text: Second: the applications and data should live on the user's computer. In MulmoClaude, the assistant's accumulation is a workspace of files. Files can be inspected, searched, copied, backed up, versioned, and moved. The model can change without losing the assistant.

left.title: Provider account
left.footer: The provider owns the switching cost

beats.text: Third: remote devices should be windows into the assistant, but the assistant's self should stay home. Today that window is the phone. In the future, it can be smart glasses, a smart headset, or whatever device is closest. The user's machine does the work. The relay carries the errand; it does not become the assistant.

slide.quote: Remote access should make each device a {primary:window}, not move the assistant into the cloud.
slide.author: The relay carries errands
slide.role: Phone today. Smart glasses, headsets, and other surfaces tomorrow.

beats.text: That is the shape of MulmoClaude. A user describes a need. The assistant grows an application. The application and data stay with the user. The phone today, and other devices later, remain windows into the same assistant.

items.title: Describe a need
items.description: the user speaks in ordinary language

items.title: Keep it yours
items.description: apps, records, and memory remain in the workspace

beats.text: The next decade will create a wide gap between people who merely rent access to powerful models, and people who have assistants that know them. The durable advantage will not be access to the smartest model. It will be an assistant whose data, applications, and learned working style belong to the person who invested in them.

slide.quote: The durable advantage is an assistant whose {primary:accumulation belongs to you}.
slide.author: Nurtured, owned, portable

beats.text: That is the vision of MulmoClaude: a place to nurture an assistant while keeping the accumulation yours. Start while it knows nothing. Let the accumulation begin on a computer that belongs to you.

slide.title: Start while it **knows nothing**
slide.subtitle: Let the accumulation begin on a computer that {primary:belongs to you}.
